content.problemStatement:
You are given an odd integer NNN and two integer sequences A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč and B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1‚Äč,B2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äč.

Your task is to reorder the elements of BBB, forming a new sequence C1,C2,‚Ä¶,CNC_1, C_2, \ldots, C_NC1‚Äč,C2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,CN‚Äč (i.e. choose a permutation P1,P2,‚Ä¶,PNP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_NP1‚Äč,P2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,PN‚Äč of the integers 111 through NNN, where Ci=BPiC_i = B_{P_i}Ci‚Äč=BPi‚Äč‚Äč for each valid iii), in such a way that the following condition holds: (A1‚äēC1)=(A2‚äēC2)=‚Ä¶=(AN‚äēCN)(A_1 \oplus C_1) = (A_2 \oplus C_2) = \ldots = (A_N \oplus C_N)(A1‚Äč‚äēC1‚Äč)=(A2‚Äč‚äēC2‚Äč)=‚Ä¶=(AN‚Äč‚äēCN‚Äč), where ‚äē\oplus‚äē denotes bitwise XOR. Find one such reordered sequence or determine that it is impossible.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1‚Äč,B2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äč.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case:


If there is no valid way to reorder the sequence BBB, print a single line containing the integer ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1.
Otherwise, print a single line containing NNN space-separated integers C1,C2,‚Ä¶,CNC_1, C_2, \ldots, C_NC1‚Äč,C2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,CN‚Äč. If there are multiple solutions, you may find any one.
